design_influences.insight: Dimmed navigation, softer separators, and compact top controls inform the quieter domain rail and low-noise panels.
A dedicated metadata side area informed the right evidence inspector and grouped separators.
Systemized spacing and careful craft informed the restrained panel geometry and type rhythm.

forbidden_inputs: unordered raw table scans with no typed lineage basis

states.inspector_copy: The failure remains active. The dashboard exposes the root chain, current owner, and next legal action from typed objects only.
states.label: Open failure
states.next_action: Open remediation and verify dominant blocking scope

closure_checks: Task closure declares effect on source error
states.inspector_copy: The remediation task is now the current_state_source, but the root error is still visible in the lineage ribbon.
states.label: Remediation active
states.next_action: Complete remediation task and declare error_resolution_effect

closure_checks: Approval basis, scope, and expiry remain visible
states.inspector_copy: Accepted risk is explicit, bounded, and reviewable. It is never a silent terminal state.
states.label: Accepted risk
states.next_action: Monitor approval expiry and accountable owner review date

closure_checks: Closure evidence and resolution_basis_ref remain queryable
Audit refs and provenance refs remain attached
Terminal posture came from typed state, not logs
states.inspector_copy: Resolution is terminal only because typed artifacts say it is. The inspector still keeps the evidence spine visible.
states.label: Resolved

query_contracts.notes: Append-only event ledger merged by recorded_at and audit_stream_ref + stream_sequence.
Joins traces, audit events, typed failures, and gate outcomes without flattening signal classes.
Shows batch allocation, selection disposition, shard claims, deferrals, and digests.
Pairs filing packet, request/response lineage, and authority reconciliation evidence.
Privacy-safe ledger of masking, legal hold, erasure, and sensitive-view events with explicit limitations.
Summarized operational briefing backed by batch audit evidence and exceptions.
Replay basis validation, comparison mode, deterministic outcome hashes, and attestation posture.
Stable graph traversal over durable nodes, edges, and lineage boundaries.
Returns proof closure, contradiction isolation, and support state for a target claim.
Traverses request, response, normalization, and reconciliation nodes without hidden joins.
Explains baseline, contradiction, supersession, and amendment justification lineage.
Explains lawful absence versus corruption through explicit limited or erased placeholders.

notes: Audit is never sampled away.
Deterministic ordering uses stream sequence, not event_time.
retention_visibility_rows.retention_boundary: Append-only durable stream; payload may degrade to HASH_ONLY or ERASED while object refs, hashes, and lineage survive.
retention_visibility_rows.visibility_boundary: Operator, reviewer, compliance, and audit investigation surfaces; customer-safe derivatives only through dedicated projection.

notes: Error spans require typed failure detail.
Sampling may not remove mandatory audit history.
retention_visibility_rows.retention_boundary: Potentially sampled operational telemetry; filing- and retention-critical spans are deterministic-retain or mandatory-forensic.
retention_visibility_rows.visibility_boundary: Operational and reliability investigation surfaces only.

notes: Metrics summarize behavior and never become proof of record.
retention_visibility_rows.retention_boundary: Aggregated metric retention shorter than audit; no privacy-reintroducing dimensions.
retention_visibility_rows.visibility_boundary: Aggregated operational, quality, and release-governance surfaces.

notes: Logs cannot reconstruct the failure dashboard or privacy ledger.
retention_visibility_rows.retention_boundary: Short-lived structured logs; may be sampled or rotated faster than audit evidence.

notes: Carries access decisions, step-up events, and session anomalies.
retention_visibility_rows.retention_boundary: Security telemetry is durable enough for investigation but still distinct from audit proof.

notes: Missing rows may not silently imply lawful erasure.
retention_visibility_rows.retention_boundary: Limited privacy telemetry does not erase explicit limitation posture.
retention_visibility_rows.visibility_boundary: Privacy-restricted access tier with customer-safe projection fences.

notes: Material failure may not degrade into free text or logs only.
retention_visibility_rows.retention_boundary: Typed failure objects persist per governed retention classes and link back to artifact_retention_ref where applicable.
retention_visibility_rows.visibility_boundary: Operator-visible detail with explicit customer_visibility_class fencing.

notes: No log-only reconstruction.
Underlying error remains visible through compensation and accepted risk.
retention_visibility_rows.retention_boundary: Persists as an authoritative read-side projection until lineage closure and retention policy allow archival.
retention_visibility_rows.visibility_boundary: Operator-facing inspection surface with filtered customer-safe derivations.

notes: Accepted risk is explicit, not silent closure.
retention_visibility_rows.retention_boundary: Retains approval lineage, bounded scope, and expiry through the life of the exception.
retention_visibility_rows.visibility_boundary: Operator, approver, tenant-admin, or security-operator visibility depending on scope.

signal_lanes.description: Append-only, legally or operationally significant, minimally editable, durable, explainability-oriented.
signal_lanes.label: Audit

signal_lanes.description: Runtime-oriented, high-volume, potentially sampled, performance and reliability-oriented, correlation-capable.
signal_lanes.label: Ops

signal_lanes.description: Access decisions, privilege changes, step-up events, binding mismatches, export attempts, and session anomalies.
signal_lanes.label: Security

signal_lanes.description: Sensitive-view access, masking actions, erasure requests, legal-hold transitions, and export or delete operations.
signal_lanes.label: Privacy

signal_laws: Audit exists to prove what happened.
Telemetry exists to explain runtime behavior.
Security and privacy signals may share infrastructure, not semantics.
Failure dashboards may not be reconstructed from logs.
